mnemonics: CAP Theorem: Choose two out of three - Consistency, Availability, Partition Tolerance. Remember: You can't have your CAke and Partition it too.
keyPoints: Consistency: Every read receives the most recent write or an error.
Availability: Every request receives a response, without guarantee it is the most recent.
Partition Tolerance: The system continues to operate despite network failures.
commonMistakes: Assuming a distributed system can achieve all three properties of the CAP theorem simultaneously.
Confusing Consistency in CAP with the C in ACID (which is about database transaction guarantees).
